sections.paragraph:
fut. יַחְשׂךְ-

(1) to restrain, to hold in. (Syr. and Chald. חֲסַךְ, ܚܣܰܟ id. A kindred root is חָזַק.) 2Sa 18:16, “Joab restrained the people” from pursuing. Pro 10:19, חֹשֵׂךְ שְׂפָתָיו “he who restrains his lips.” Job 7:11 16:5 Isa 58:1, “cry aloud (with the throat), אַל־תַּחְשֹׂוךְ keep not back (thy throat or mouth).” Followed by מִן to restrain from something, Gen 20:6 1Sa 25:39 2Sa 18:16.

Hence-(2) to preserve, to keep safely from something, Pro 24:11 Psa 78:50 Job 33:18 and-

(3) to withhold something from any one, i.e. to deny it to him; followed by מִן of the person, and acc. of the thing, Gen 39:9 22:12 but verse 16 Gen 39:16without מִן of person.

(4) to spare, to be sparing of-(a) things (Germ. fparen). Pro 13:24, “he who spares the rod hates his son;” 11:24 21:26 .-(b) men (Germ. fchonen). Isa 14:6 2Ki 5:20. Followed by לְ to reserve for something (für etwas auffparen), Job 38:23.

Niphal-

(1) to be restrained, pass. of No. 1, Job 16:6.

(2) pass. of No. 4, to be reserved for any thing, Job 21:30.
